## **About the Report** 

TCS’ Integrated Annual Report for FY 2025-26 (FY 2026) covers the financial and nonfinancial performance during the year, and provides information about its strategy, business outlook, governance practices, material issues, risks, and opportunities. 

###### **Reporting Period** 

###### **Integrated Business Model** 

###### **Materiality** 

###### **Exclusions** 

###### **REPORTING FRAMEWORKS** 

This report covers the financial year TCS’s ability to create long-term value April 1, 2025 to March 31, 2026. Unless is interrelated and fundamentally stated otherwise, all data, performance dependent on various forms of capitals metrics, and strategic disclosures relate available (inputs), their usage (valueto this period. accretive activities), the Company’s impact on each of the capitals and the **Reporting Entity** value delivered, and impact managed (outputs and outcomes). 

This Report covers information on the Indian and international operations of TCS Limited (Parent Company) and its subsidiaries which are collectively referred to as the ‘Company’. 

Financial Intellectual Capital Capital Human Social Capital Capital 

###### **Integrated Reporting** 

This Report goes beyond financial reporting to cover TCS’s non-financial performance, opportunities, risks and outcomes attributable to or associated with key stakeholders, which have a significant influence on the Company’s ability to create value.

This Report focuses on information that is material to the Company’s business. It provides a concise overview of the Company’s performance, prospects, and ability to provide sustainable value for all its stakeholders. The legitimate interests of all stakeholders have been considered and all material information has been included in this Report. 

###### **Boundary** 

This report includes the sustainability performance across TCS’ global owned and operated locations. Disclosures extending to the value chain or select regions are specified where applicable. The financial boundary follows the consolidated Group reporting framework. 

###### **Audit and Assurance** 

TCS’ financial statements have been audited by B S R & Co. LLP, Chartered Accountants. Select non-financial data within the BRSR has been subject to reasonable and limited assurance by KPMG, an independent third-party assurance provider. The scope and conclusions of these engagements are set out in the respective assurance statements. 

All financial figures and metrics presented in this report are stated after excluding exceptional items. In FY 2026, the Company incurred certain non-recurring items, comprising of restructuring charges, provision towards a legal claim, and the impact of the new Labour Code in India. These have been treated as exceptional items and have been excluded from financial information and related explanations in management commentary and other non-statutory sections to provide a clearer view of underlying business performance, unless otherwise indicated in those sections. The Consolidated and Standalone Financial Statements continue to be reported in accordance with applicable Indian Accounting Standards.

Please share your feedback on this report at: <u>investor.relations@tcs.com</u>

## **About TCS** 

###### **Leadership in Enterprise AI - From Infrastructure to Intelligence** 

Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) is the technology partner of choice for industryleading organisations worldwide. Since its inception in 1968, TCS has upheld the highest standards of innovation, engineering excellence and customer centricity. 

The TCS’ FY 2026 Integrated Annual Report reflects its aspiration and investments across the full AI stack from AI Data centre to delivering Agentic AI-led transformation. The stack is structured across five integrated layers, infrastructure, tools, data, models, and applications, providing the essential 

building blocks required to design, deploy the AI Stack allows intelligence to and operationalise AI securely and at be seamlessly integrated into core scale. By bringing together resilient enterprise processes. This approach digital infrastructure, standardised AI enables enterprises to move from and engineering tools, trusted data technology readiness to insight-driven foundations, a mix of foundational transformation, embedding AI and domain-specific models, and responsibly into decision-making, AI-embedded business applications, operations, and value creation. 

Rooted in the heritage of the Tata Group, TCS is focused on creating long term value for its clients, its investors, its employees, and the community at large. With a highly skilled workforce of over 580,000 consultants spread across 56 countries and 194 service delivery centers across the world, the company has been recognised as a top employer in six continents. With the ability to rapidly apply and scale new technologies, the Company has built long term partnerships with its clients. Many of these relationships have endured into decades and navigated every technology cycle, from mainframes in the 1970s to Artificial Intelligence today. 

TCS sponsors 14 of the world’s most prestigious marathons and endurance events, including TCS New York City Marathon, TCS London Marathon, Tata Mumbai Marathon, and TCS Sydney Marathon, with a focus on promoting health, sustainability, and community empowerment. 

TCS generated consolidated revenues of US $30 billion in the fiscal year ended March 31, 2026. 

For more information, visit: 

**www.tcs.com** 

A historic moment at the 2026 TCS London Marathon, as Sebastian Sawe became the first athlete to run a sub-two-hour marathon, in a world-record time of 1:59:30. 

The Company has set an aspiration to become the **world’s largest AI-led technology services company** and is enabling its clients to transform themselves across the full AI stack, from infrastructure to intelligence.

## **Letter from the Chairman** 

We are in the midst of intensifying geopolitical tensions, shifts in trade and 

**AI is now becoming the infrastructure of intelligence. It will influence how enterprises invest, organize supply chains, manage risk, and serve stakeholders. GenAI and agentic AI are entering a new phase of growth. AI will not just be a layer but will be the operating foundation.** 

###### **N Chandrasekaran Dear Shareholders,** Chairman 

**It is my privilege to present to you the Integrated Annual Report 2025-26 of Tata Consultancy Services for the financial year ended March 31, 2026.** 

We are in the midst of intensifying geopolitical tensions, shifts in trade and supply chains, and uneven economic growth. Across markets, corporates are prioritising resilience, productivity and trust based decisions. 

Fiscal 2026 performance saw strong client metrics with healthy additions across every revenue band, validating the critical role of the company in clients’ transformation journey. Clients generating over US$ 100 million revenue annually rose by 2 to 66, and the US$ 50 million revenue band increased by 9 to 139 clients. Your company signed several large deals including 5 mega deals in the year resulting in a strong demand. The total contract value exceeded US$ 40 bn. 

The year 2026 began with expectations of steady global growth, soft inflation and easier financial conditions. The year witnessed positive developments including the signing of the landmark India-EU trade agreement and the interim India-US trade deal. However, by early March, the start of the West Asia crisis brought rising concerns about stagflation—falling output coupled with rising inflation. 

In this backdrop, your company delivered a strong performance with the fiscal 2026 consolidated revenue of ₹ 267,021 crore, delivering a growth of 4.6% over the previous year. The Operating Income was ₹ 66,838 crore with an Operating Margin of 25.0%. The Net Income was ₹ 52,820 crore, a growth of 8.8% over the previous year, with the Net Margin of 19.8%. Both the Operating Margin and Net Margin were the highest over the past four years. The cash flows from operations were 105.9% of the net profit. 

The Board has recommended a final dividend of H 31/- per share, subject to your approval. The total dividend is ₹110/- per share for Fiscal 2026. 

led transformation. For a European airline, we modernised a mission critical crew management system using GenAI, rebuilding decades of legacy logic. For a leading U.S. utility, AI driven dispatch optimisation improved field efficiency by 30%. For an electronics manufacturer, Physical AI is now improving safety and productivity. 

AI is a foundational technology that cuts across all industries. The advancements in AI are creating industry wide shifts towards automation and transformation. 

Your company has created a comprehensive AI strategy to provide full stack AI solutions to customers across industries. TCS is repositioning itself as an AI & Digital transformation partner. In fiscal 2026, TCS scaled up execution of the ‘Human+AI’ operating model and industry specific agent marketplace. Your company generated annualised revenues of US$ 2.3 billion in AI Services and US$ 11.5 billion in new-age services including Cloud, Data, Enterprise Solutions and Cyber Security. 

As AI scales, key aspects of trust, security, and data sovereignty will be non-negotiable. Enterprises will need an AI Operating System. This Operating System will be a foundation of infrastructure, data, models, context, agents, and governance. Building it demands deep industry expertise, partnerships, and a clear understanding of enterprise context. 

During the year, we launched HyperVault a secure by design AI infrastructure platform, in partnership with TPG and supported by a deep partner ecosystem. As global constraints on power, compute, and geography intensify, India is emerging as a strategic ‘+1’ destination for AI infrastructure. HyperVault positions us at the centre of this shift. 

Looking ahead, your Company will prioritize efforts across four focus areas: 

- Build AI Operating System for industries, to accelerate deployment of agentic AI solutions 

TCS is also focusing on acquiring and scaling next-gen capabilities through • Construct India’s first AI focused inorganic initiatives. This year your data centre, with rack density Company completed acquisition of two greater than 160 KW companies with deep expertise in AI, cloud, and SaaS. TCS announced the acquisition • Strengthen our Infrastructure of Coastal Cloud, for US$ 700 million in to Intelligence offering with December 2025, and ListEngage in October 360<sup>0</sup> partnerships with 2025. These will boost TCS’ Salesforce hyperscalers, frontier AI firms and practice and agentic AI capabilities. industrial OEMs 

- Strengthen our Infrastructure to Intelligence offering with 360<sup>0</sup> partnerships with hyperscalers, frontier AI firms and industrial OEMs 

- Establish secure, resilient and sovereign AI infrastructure 

AI is now becoming the infrastructure of intelligence. It will influence how enterprises invest, organize supply chains, manage risk, and serve stakeholders. GenAI and agentic AI are entering a new phase of growth. AI will not just be a layer but will be the operating foundation. Customers will move from pilots to scale, embedding AI into core functions. 

As we build a future ready TCS, we will lead with purpose, innovate responsibly, and uphold stakeholder’s trust in everything we do. This is anchored in the Tata value system—integrity, responsibility, inclusion, and respect for every individual. 

Guided by Project Aalingana, the Tata Group’s vision for a greener, cleaner and more equitable future, sustainability remains integral to your company. In Fiscal 2026, nearly 79% of our global energy consumption was sourced from renewables, re-affirming our commitment to sustainable operations. 

During the year, your company helped over 18.13 million people worldwide through our programmes in education, skills, healthcare, and the environment. Women, youth, and marginalised groups gained new opportunities. The TCS Literacy and Women Empowerment Programmes supported rural India helping the youth find jobs and learning digital skills. Your company’s employees volunteered 9.42 million hours, impacting 12.96 million lives. 

I thank our customers, partners, and shareholders for their trust. I thank our associates for their commitment and ingenuity. 

**As technology reshapes the world, our responsibility is clear - innovate with integrity, scale with trust, and grow with humanity.** 

###### **Warm regards,** 

**N Chandrasekaran** 

Chairman

## **Letter from the CEO** 

**Our unique advantage combines a deep understanding of enterprise context, a proven track record in industry-leading execution, ability to reskill our workforce rapidly and at scale, strong ecosystem partnerships, and leadership in AI-driven services.** 

Dear Shareholders, 

**Over the years your company has N Chandrasekaran put exceptional focus on client-** Chairman **centricity. We have earned the trust of over 670 global clients who worked with us for more than ten years, including over 250 clients with relationships spanning over twenty years. Our unique advantage combines a deep understanding of enterprise context, a proven track record in industry-leading execution, the ability to reskill our workforce rapidly and at scale, strong ecosystem partnerships, and leadership in AI-driven services.** 

adoption, further influenced leadership decision making. In this context, the role of trusted system integrators became even more vital, with clients increasingly choosing partners who bring strong enterprise and industry context and are willing to take end-to-end accountability for business outcomes and ROI. 

willingness to commit to long-term, multiyear, multi-million-dollar partnerships— reflecting the trust they place in TCS to deliver certainty and value at scale over extended transformation journeys. 130 of our top 139 clients generating over US$ 50 million in annual revenues, have chosen your company as the AI services partner. 

I am pleased to share with you that our clients further deepened their partnership with TCS to help them achieve their business outcomes. Global clients partnered with us to improve business resilience, optimize costs, transform operating models for agility, reduce technology debt through modernization and scale AI across the enterprise. 

We delivered complex, large-scale programs across geographies, including nation-scale outcomes in India. We scaled the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) into the world’s largest government e-commerce marketplace, with procurement exceeding ₹ 5 lakh crore. For instance, we upgraded State Bank of India’s YONO 2.0 to serve 20 crore users in 10 languages. We also modernised citizen services such as ePassports (over 1 

In FY 2026, global enterprises continued to navigate an increasingly complex macro-economic and geo-political environment. The rapid evolution of AI and the need to accelerate its enterprise 

This translated into broad-based client additions across revenue bands, strong order book, and continued momentum in large deals. Clients showed greater 

14 

crore issued) and built the RBI’s new data management system, designed to handle 250 TB for sharper economic analysis. 

Our execution has been recognised globally. Newsweek ranked TCS #1 among Technology Services enterprises in America’s Most Reliable Companies, and Fortune listed us among the World’s Most Admired Companies in 2026. 

FY 2026 marked an inflection point for enterprise AI. For the first time since GenAI emerged in 2023, customers moved decisively from pilots to scaled deployments. AI became central to every conversation and every solution, accelerating adoption across industries. 

This year has been pivotal for TCS. To realise the opportunities offered by AI, we set out a bold aspiration **to become the world’s largest AI-led technology services company** . Our strategy to realize this aspiration, is to be a full-stack AI services player **from Infrastructure to Intelligence** . We have identified five transformation pillars that will underpin this strategy. I am pleased to share that we made strong progress across all five pillars: 

- **1) tcs**<sup>**AI**</sup> **:** We democratised AI access for all associates, ran the world’s largest hackathon with over 281,000 participants, and institutionalised ‘AI Friday hackathons’ to build a continuous culture of learning and innovation. We also transformed internal operations through a customer-zero approach. 

- **2) Redefining Services:** We established a dedicated Services Transformation unit and launched a services re-design framework based on a Human + AI service autonomy model. We are taking AI-led, re-designed services proactively to existing clients and new prospects, enabling consistent deployment at enterprise scale. 

###### **3) Building a Future-Ready Talent** 

**Model:** As AI reshapes roles and ways of working, we are acting ahead of the curve. In FY 2026, we invested 69 million learning hours and enabled 5.2 million 

competencies to be acquired. Over 270,000 employees now have advanced AI skills-3x last year. We continue to hire globally for next-gen roles (cybersecurity, cloud, AI, data), with over 50% of experienced hires bringing advanced skills. Our Initial Learning Programme now integrates GenAI, giving every trainee access to leading platforms and hands-on learning. 

**4) Making AI Real for our clients:** True value of AI can be realised by enterprises only when they are able to reimagine their business value chains leveraging AI to innovate their products, services and customer experiences. This requires them to modernise their core applications and data estate. We have created an AI acceleration playbook: **_Innovate with AI, Build with AI, Scale with AI_ ,** and deployed it across a significant number of customers to solve high-value problems in rapid 12–16 week cycles. 

**5) AI ecosystem play:** To strengthen our differentiation and competitive edge, we must be deeply embedded in the AI ecosystem and operate with both speed and scale. To do this, we are following a **Build-Partner-Acquire** approach. Alongside continued organic investment in our capabilities, we will deepen partnerships with leading AI ecosystem players, acquire world-class organizations, and launch new ventures. In FY 2026 we, 

• Expanded strategic collaborations with AMD, OpenAI, ABB, Honeywell, ServiceNow, Cisco, GitLab, Zscaler, Google Cloud, and others, accelerating innovation and staying ahead of evolving client needs. 

- Acquired ListEngage and Coastal Cloud to deepen Salesforce and AI consulting, positioning us among the top five global Salesforce consultants. 

- Launched HyperVault, our AI Infrastructure business with TPG, with an initial plan to build 1GW of data centre capacity in India. 

Since launch, we have secured customer commitments, finalised land parcels, and progressed partnering agreements. We are engaging hyperscalers, semiconductor companies, and model providers, while we serve as the integration partner across infrastructure, engineering, and AI-led services. 

**Looking ahead** , as customers scale AI, they are looking for partners who can build and manage a unified control plane across the full AI stack – Infrastructure to Intelligence. This control plane - an **AI Operating System** - makes AI repeatable and governable through orchestration in an enterprise’s context and embeds trust mechanisms such as security, monitoring, evaluation, and auditability. Many enterprises have tools and models, but not the standardised control and context needed to run agentic systems safely in day-to-day operations today. 

This is where TCS is positioning itself as an **Enterprise Intelligence Integrator** , modernising software stacks, embedding AI agents into operations, and governing data and security at scale to deliver measurable outcomes. 

As outlined by our Chairman, we will focus on four distinct progressions: Build industry-specific AI systems, construct India’s first high-density AI data centre, strengthen our Infrastructure to Intelligence offering with 360<sup>0</sup> partnerships and establish secure, resilient, sovereign AI infrastructure. 

I thank our customers for their trust, our associates for their dedication and delivery excellence, and you—our shareholders—for your unwavering support. With our execution strength, deep context, and a clear AI-led strategy, we are well positioned to navigate uncertainty and compound value. We will stay focused on resilient growth, strong governance, and consistent returns. 

###### **Best regards,** 

###### **K Krithivasan** 

Chief Executive Officer and Managing Director

## **The Enterprise AI Opportunity** 

**Each technology wave has expanded role of System Integrators to help customers infuse new technology and derive maximum value** 

The advent of generative AI marks a pivotal moment in technological history, creating a new frontier for enterprise innovation and value creation. 

While awareness is high, AI adoption remains at an early stage, with 95% of enterprises still in the initial phases of their AI journey. This leaves significant room for growth, scale and transformation across industries. 

Enterprise AI represents a once-ina-generation opportunity to reshape how work gets done, driving stepchange productivity, accelerating cost optimisation and enabling end-to-end business reimagination across industries. 

Enterprises are moving from being digital-driven to becoming AI-driven. The leap from a digital enterprise to an AI enterprise is about harnessing the full potential of AI - not just as a tool, but as a strategic partner that empowers people, transforms processes, and redefines what is possible. 

###### **95% enterprises are in early Al adoption phase** 

An AI Enterprise, 

Tech potential 

- Uses data and context to guide every decision. 

5% 

- Applies advanced analytics and reasoning to generate deeper insights and better decisions. 

- Automates processes increasingly, enabling AI systems to act independently. 

40% 

55% 

- Runs on intelligent software that can adapt and learn. 

- Treats AI as a co-architect of choice, helping shape strategy and outcomes. 

Successful AI adoption requires addressing technology debt, ensuring data readiness, and simplifying operating models. 

AI is not just another technology wave; it is reshaping how enterprises create, consume, and scale intelligence. 

As clients move from experiments to scaled adoption, they increasingly seek partners with deep enterprise and industry expertise who can take end-toend accountability for business outcomes and ROI. As a result, trusted System Integrators play an even more critical role.

System Integrators need to reimagine how they deliver value to address enterprise challenges in the Al era 

02 Getting enterprises AI-ready by modernising their technology landscape and strengthening their data, cloud, and cybersecurity foundations. Value 

Redesigning traditional products and services with AI-powered IT operations, autonomous business processes, and intelligent software development. 

This creates a compelling runway for full-stack AI services and for new revenue streams spanning platforms and emerging capabilities. System Integrators that move early, build trusted ecosystems, and invest in talent and operating-model transformation will be best positioned to emerge as winners, as enterprises consolidate vendors and prioritise outcomes at scale. 

Transforming industry value chains through AI-led reimagination of core processes such as claims processing and drug discovery. 

Building new revenue streams through disruptive innovations such as AI data centers and next-generation robotics and humanoids. 

Taking this into consideration, TCS has set out a bold aspiration to become the world’s largest AI-led technology services company. TCS has built a strategy to become a full-stack AI services player - from Infrastructure to Intelligence. That strategy is anchored in five transformation pillars. 

With this clearly outlined strategy and investments, TCS has a comprehensive approach to turn the promise of AI into reality for its clients. 

The market will reward System Integrators who can redesign traditional delivery into a Human+AI model, lead foundational programs (data foundations, cloud and tech modernisation), and then industrialise transformation across value chains.

## **Full Stack AI Services: Infrastructure to Intelligence** 

Enterprise adoption of AI is rapidly moving from experimentation to scaled deployment. This shift requires seamless integration across multiple layers—spanning infrastructure, data, models, platforms, applications, and real-world execution. Increasingly, clients are seeking a single, accountable partner who can orchestrate this entire stack with scale, security, and deep industry context. 

For TCS, this end-to-end capability is a strategic differentiator. It enables TCS to move beyond isolated AI interventions to owning transformation outcomes—embedding intelligence into core operations and delivering sustained business value. 

###### **TCS’ Full Stack AI Services strategy is built across five integrated layers:** 

###### **Infrastructure to Intelligence** 

Intelligence in Action

TCS AMR, Quadruped Robotics & Smart devices Third party integration services Conversational & Visual interfaces

###### **03** 

As AI reshapes the way work is performed, the workforce of the future must continuously learn, adapt and lead with an AI-first mindset. Building a future-ready talent model therefore remains a key pillar of TCS’ AI-led transformation, anchored around three core elements — skills, roles and talent. 

###### **Future ready Skills** 

AI-native talent 

Building future-ready skills begins with AI fluency at scale. TCS has invested extensively to enable its workforce to work alongside AI. Over 270,000 associates now possess advanced proficiencies in AI and machine learning. This transformation is further strengthened by AI-native graduates, whose intuitive use of AI complements experienced professionals and reinforces an AI-first culture. 

###### **AI-powered learning innovations, including the Personalised Learning Coach, accelerate application-led skilling.** 

As of March 31, 2026, more than 270,000 associates possess higher -order AI/ML skills, supported by 69 million learning hours and 186,000 external certifications. 

###### **Future ready Roles** 

Redefining roles 

Every role across the organisation from sales and advisory to solutioning and delivery is becoming AI-centric. Redefined AI and data career streams has provided clear role definitions and pathways for deep specialisation, enabling associates in any role to evolve as AI practitioners and champions. This transformation is reinforced through enterprise-wide initiatives such as AI Fridays, which institutionalise continuous learning and experimentation. These initiatives encourage teams to explore AI use cases, share innovations and accelerate AI adoption in real time. 

Programmes such as AI Dojo equip teams to lead AI-driven customer conversations, while new roles, including rapid-build engineers and leads, reflect evolving delivery models. 

###### **Future ready Talent** 

Securing top talent 

TCS has strengthened advisory and consulting capabilities across key growth areas such as cybersecurity, cloud, and enterprise platforms. Today, over 50% of experienced hires bring next-generation skills, positioning TCS to lead confidently into its AI-driven future. 

This talent strategy is further supported by AI-enabled hiring, curated talent marketplaces and partnerships with 181 academic institutions, helping TCS attract world-class talent. These efforts are complemented by flagship initiatives such as CodeVita<sup>TM</sup> and HackQuest. 

###### **TCS Recognized as a Leader in Talent Readiness** 

TCS has been named a **Leader in Everest Group’s Talent Readiness for Next-Generation Application Services** PEAK Matrix® 2025. This accolade validates TCS’ strategic commitment to building a future-ready, skill-based workforce, highlighting the Company’s robust learning infrastructure and pioneering efforts in fostering Human+ AI collaboration. 

**04** 

With rapid technological advancements, enterprises face challenges in keeping up with AI and digital adoption in their context. Introducing a new technology in enterprises requires deep understanding of business and technology landscape, and making the new technology work in this context. To implement AI in enterprise context, there is a need to integrate AI and Agents into workflows, reimagine processes and integrate AI with enterprise data and systems. 

Enterprises are prioritising technology investments across four key transformation areas, creating a compelling opportunity for TCS to make AI real for its clients. 

###### **Digital foundation** 

Getting AI-ready 

Enterprises recognise that scaling AI requires a strong digital foundation. TCS helps clients prepare their enterprise core for AI by modernising cloud and infrastructure, strengthening cybersecurity, upgrading enterprise systems, and building resilient AI-ready data platforms. This technology foundation is critical to deploy AI securely, reliably, and at scale. 

###### **AI-Led modernisation** 

Eliminating Technology debt 

AI presents a unique opportunity to address long-standing technology debt that enterprises have often deferred due to cost, complexity and long duration of such initiatives. Through its AI-powered modernisation workbench, Code Plus, TCS is helping clients modernise their enterprise technology stacks with greater speed and certainty. Supported by reusable patterns and an integrated agent marketplace, Code Plus is accelerating modernisation initiatives and enabling enterprises to transform legacy environments more efficiently. 

###### **AI-led reimagination** 

Accelerating Enterprise adoption 

AI presents a significant opportunity for enterprises to drive superior business outcomes by reimagining end-to-end industry value chains. This requires deep understanding of industry and enterprise context, modern technology stack, full stack integration capabilities, ecosystem partnerships and a robust domain specific transformation approach. Clients are increasingly seeking partners capable of bringing these capabilities together to deliver integrated and scalable transformation outcomes.

**Innovate with AI** – AI Immersion sessions bring together business and technology teams together to gain AI-fluency and identify high-impact business problems best suited for AI-led solutions, with a clear proof-to-value approach.

## **AI with Purpose India AI Impact Summit 2026** 

At the India AI Impact Summit 2026, TCS conducted one of India’s largest singlesession AI training initiatives, upskilling over 2,000 students. The Tata Bharat YUVAi Hackathon was conceived with a clear vision: to democratise access to artificial intelligence and enable students from non engineering backgrounds to identify real problems in their own fields and build working digital solutions using AI tools in their own mother tongue without any prior coding experience. The initiative proved that domain expertise, curiosity, and access to AI in native languages are sufficient to create meaningful AI-led solutions. 

Beyond enterprise transformation, TCS remained committed to societal progress. 

At the India AI Impact Summit 2026, TCS showcased its unique capability to scale AI responsibly, from foundational infrastructure to real-world, societal impact. Through live programs, hands-on immersions, and large-scale participation, TCS demonstrated its commitment to making AI accessible, human-centered, and inclusive at a population scale. This effort reflects TCS’ belief that AI leadership is defined not just by technological depth, but by who it empowers and how effectively it transforms lives. 

The programme operated through a distributed format, with satellite hackathons conducted over six weeks across 22 colleges in 10 states, engaging more than 10,000 students ahead of the India AI Impact Summit 2026. Participants used voice-first, multilingual AI tools available in nine Indian languages, progressing from problem identification to working prototypes within 90–120 minutes. 

Building on this strong foundation, the Tata AI Sakhi initiative is set to scale through broader regional outreach, expanded mentorship networks, and deeper ecosystem partnerships, with the goal of empowering thousands of women as AI-enabled entrepreneurs and agents of change across India. 

empowering women from rural India to confidently use AI as a practical tool for economic independence and self-reliance. The initiative successfully transformed AI from an abstract concept to a tangible tool, enabling women to integrate it meaningfully into their daily lives and livelihoods. 

At the India AI Impact Summit 2026, the Tata AI Sakhi Immersion Programme brought the Tata Group’s vision of making AI a force for inclusion to life. The programme’s impact was immediate and measurable: in just 2.5 hours, 1,553 women successfully completed 4,727 AI-powered tasks. 

The programme followed an immersive, hands-on format. Participants, or ‘Sakhis’, from rural India were organised into structured cohorts based on their digital proficiency and supported by a high-touch mentorship framework, with one mentor assigned for every five women. Using their own mobile phones and in their local languages, the Sakhis explored practical, real-world AI applications—from design ideation and marketing content creation to accessing government schemes and drafting business communications. The results were significant, with advanced cohorts achieved a 98% task completion rate, demonstrating high engagement and rapid development of capability and confidence. 

What set YUVAi apart was its strong focus on inclusivity. By removing barriers related to coding knowledge, English proficiency and prior technical exposure, the initiative enabled broad-based participation while delivering consistent outcomes at national scale. The programme created thousands of first-time AI builders, validated a multilingual operating framework and established a growing ecosystem of mentors. 

**I have come here today to feel the energy that you all have brought with you. The way you all are using technology to create new solutions, that’s phenomenal. What this proves is that AI can be used by everyone for solving your day-today problems, for creating solutions that you need, and you understand.** 

The success at the summit laid the foundation for scaling YUVAi into a sustained, pan India talent development platform. 

**When technology speaks the language of people and addresses their real needs, it becomes a tool of dignity and empowerment rather than exclusion.** 

###### **Ashwini Vaishnaw** 

Honourable Minister of Railways, Information and Broadcasting, and Electronics and Information Technology 

**Smriti Irani**

## **Customer Stories** 

##### Lloyds Banking Group - Re-imagining Banking with AI 

**Lloyds Banking Group** , one of the UK’s largest financial services institutions, is on a mission to reimagine banking by embedding AI across every aspect of the enterprise. With a strong focus on delivering personalised, efficient, and secure banking experiences, the Group is scaling AI to transform customer engagement, product innovation, engineering productivity, colleague support, and core operations. 

from concept to production for AI powered solutions, and ensure GenAI adoption delivered measurable business value in terms of efficiency, innovation, and growth. 

With its extensive AI & Data expertise and its deep contextual knowledge of customer landscape, Lloyds Banking Group partnered with TCS in establishing a GenAI office to drive enterprise-wide AI adoption, following a thorough assessment of bank’s business & technology stack to define the architecture & roadmap for GenAI implementation. 

To accelerate this journey and move from experimentation to enterprise-wide impact, Lloyds Banking Group partnered with TCS. 

This provides an environment for colleagues to self-serve GenAI products and models, enabling development from concept to production, supported by frameworks, and guardrails to ensure scalable, safe and cost-efficient development of GenAI products. This has significantly helped the bank in not only scaling GenAI adoption, but also in decentralisation & democratisation of AI across the enterprise. 

As Generative AI emerged as a transformative technology, Lloyds Banking Group recognised the opportunity to unlock significant value— but also the complexity of scaling it across a large, regulated organisation. 

The bank needed to move beyond siloed AI initiatives to enterprise-wide adoption, enable self service innovation, establish robust guardrails, accelerate the journey 

With the GenAI Office in place, Lloyds Banking Group is well positioned to realise enterprise-wide value from AI: 

- Enhanced customer experiences 

- Increased engineering efficiency 

- • Stronger colleague enablement • Operational efficiencies • Revenue growth opportunities

Most importantly, the bank now has a future-ready AI foundation that enables continuous innovation as GenAI technologies and business needs evolve.

Our collaboration with TCS has been instrumental in the successful launch of the GenAI workbench.**

Air France-KLM’s Journey from Infrastructure to Intelligence

ecosystem. As the national backbone for health claims exchange, NHCX enables real-time, transparent, and efficient data sharing between providers and payers across India. With the rollout of NHCX, TCS-supported systems are shifting health claims processing from siloed workflows to an interoperable, standards-based 

**Beneficiaries treated so far** 

#### 10.67 crores 

###### **Reimagining Citizen Services in India** 

The **Passport Seva Programme** is a global benchmark in e-governance delivered through a public-private partnership. In 2025-26, TCS successfully rolled out the **PSP 2.0** application across 37 Regional Passport Offices and 200 missions. It is a future-ready, resilient, automated, and intelligent system refresh with improved reliability that enables the issuance of e-Passports to Indian citizens worldwide. Each e-Passport contains an embedded RFID chip and antenna storing the holder’s demographic and biometric data. The chip is powered by the reader’s electromagnetic field and does not require a battery. The e-passport rollout is expected to deter identity theft, forgery, and enhance connectivity for a streamlined immigration process. 

Daily service volume through PSP2.0 application 

Passport Seva Kendras 

#### 93 

> <sup>Citizens</sup> 65,000+ 

Post Office PSKs 

e-Passports issued since rollout 

450+ 1.5 crore+ 

Serviced by Software: An AI-led operating model for BFSI platforms 

This model has delivered sustained, measurable impact. The AI-led contact centre has reduced after-call work by 40% and cut call quality assessment and audit effort by 80%, while improving data integrity and removing human bias from call analysis and scoring. Early-warning and complaints-propensity models have driven proactive intervention, contributing to a 10% reduction in complaints and lower rework. AI-assisted complaint response generation has also reduced turnaround times from hours to minutes. 

###### **TCS BFSI Products & Platforms is a leading solutions provider for the financial services industry.** 

TCS designs, builds, operates, and continuously modernises these services to meet changing customer expectations and rapid technology shifts. 

In the UK, TCS supports more than 35 million life and pension policyholders, including through Diligenta, TCS’s wholly owned, FCA-regulated subsidiary. Together, TCS and Diligenta deliver BPaaS for leading life and pension providers including **Standard Life, Aviva, Lloyds Banking Group, M&G, Nest, and NOW: Pensions.** 

Over the past 18 months, TCS has embedded AI across the service value chain to create a next-generation ‘serviced by software’ model— now supporting more than 25 million customers. The platform uses over 100 AI agents across contact centres, back-office operations, customer communications, and complaints management, enabling capabilities such as speech recognition, call summarisation, complaint prediction, resolution-letter drafting, intelligent email and secure-message handling, and personalised customer journeys. 

TCS’ end-to-end servicing platform combines **TCS BaNCS for Insurance, Intelligent Experience (IEX), and Quartz** to deliver policy administration, customer service, and digital transformation. 

Collectively, these capabilities have increased service speed for policyholders and intermediaries while creating a more scalable, cost-effective operating model.

## **The Year Gone By** 

Quarter 4 

Renewed a multi-year strategic partnership with **Marks & Spencer** to transform the Company to become an omnichannel, data-driven retailer supported by globally best-in-class modern technologies. TCS will utilise AI in its transformation approach, supported by its extensive domain expertise and one of the world’s largest AI talent pools. This will support future-readiness, provide the right base for long-term growth, and create superior customer experience. 

Chairman of the Tata Group **N Chandrasekaran** has been presented the insignia of the **Knight Commander of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (KBE)** in recognition of his services to strengthen UK-India business relations. 

Signed a multi-year partnership with **ServiceNow** for developing trusted, AI powdered solutions on the ServiceNow platform to reimagine back office functions such as HR, finance, supply chain, procurement, and employee services. 

Formed a strategic partnership with **Honeywell** to advance building operations from traditional automation to enterprise autonomy by combining their expertise in IT, cloud, operational technology, and AI analytics for greater efficiency. 

Collaborated with **AMD** to help enterprises scale AI adoption from pilots to production, modernise legacy environments, and build secure, high performance digital workplaces. 

Launched **Rapid Outcome AI Platform,** built on **NVIDIA** , helping enterprises accelerate their journey from AI experimentation to scalable, real-world business outcomes. 

Partnered with **OpenAI** to drive AI-powered innovation across enterprise, consumer, and social sectors. This partnership spans multiple high-impact areas, including powering AI-led innovation across Tata Group companies, jointly accelerating AI transformation across industries globally, and establishing AI infrastructure with an initial 100 MW capacity, with an option to scale to 1 GW. This infrastructure will power next-generation AI workloads and position India as a global AI hub. 

Inaugurated **Gemini Experience Center** (GEC) at TCS’ Innovation Hub in Troy, Michigan, enabling manufacturers to co-innovate and scale cutting-edge Physical AI solutions for smarter, resilient operations. The centre forms part of the global expansion of TCS’ GEC network, which now includes centres in Bangalore, New York, Chennai, Riyadh, Singapore and São Paulo. 

34 

Expanded strategic collaboration with **Zscaler** to deliver secure, AI-driven digital workplace experiences for the modern enterprise. 

TCS strengthened its strategic collaboration with **ABB** across IT, AI and engineering services. The Company also extended its 18-year partnership to implement AI-driven IT operations globally for ABB, headquartered in Switzerland. 

At the **India AI Impact Summit** , TCS and the Tata Group showcased a full-stack AI story - from ‘Infrastructure to Intelligence’- anchored by a large-scale AI Pavilion that engaged a wide audience both on-ground and digitally. Together, they also advanced AI adoption and inclusion through practical AI masterclasses across four tracks, including robotics, financial services, and public services. 

TCS and **Cisco** launched a **Center of Excellence** in Hyderabad for Autonomous Enterprise Operations. The initiative is designed to help enterprises transition from rule-based automation to intelligent, self-governing operations capable of understanding real-time context and taking autonomous actions. 

Established a collaboration with **GitLab Inc.,** the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps, to combine TCS’ expertise in AI-led enterprise transformation capabilities with GitLab’s pre-built AI agents and intelligent orchestration for software development. 

As a longstanding strategic partner to the **World Economic Forum** , TCS had a significant presence at their annual meeting in Davos, Switzerland. This included the flagship TCS reception attended by world leaders, CXO roundtables in partnership with academia partners **MIT and CMU** on advancing the strategic deployment of AI in enterprises, a **‘Future Leaders’** session in partnership with **Pearson** and a roundtable for CEOs of the Semiconductor and AI ecosystem. TCS demonstrated a Generative AI showcase at its Tata Tea stall at the event, with an AI Avatar flavorist who helped people determine which type of tea was best suited for them. During the event, the Chairman of the Tata Group and the Chief Minister of Maharashtra also jointly announced a new ‘Innovation City’ in Mumbai and a strategic partnership to develop and implement a circular economy plan for making the city more sustainable, driven by an AI-first approach.

Secured $1 billion investment from **TPG** , a leading global alternative asset management firm to accelerate the growth of its AI Data Center business, **HyperVault** . This investment will support HyperVault’s GW-scale AI-ready infrastructure build and aligns with TCS’ plan to create AI-ready data centers with capacity in excess of a GW over the next few years. 

Signed a definitive agreement in December 2025 to acquire 100% stake in **Coastal Cloud** , a leading Salesforce Summit partner that specialises in Salesforce advisory and business consulting capabilities across verticals globally. Earlier, in October 2025, TCS strengthened its Salesforce practice with its **ListEngage** acquisition, a Summit Partner recognised for expertise in Agentforce, Marketing Cloud and Commerce Cloud. With the acquisitions of ListEngage and Coastal Cloud, TCS will be among top 5 Salesforce advisory and consulting firm, globally. 

Developed **AI-Powered AWS Innovations** to accelerate enterprise modernisation and transformation, showcasing these capabilities at various AWS Summits in 2025. The portfolio included TCS Adaptive AI Cloud Suite on AWS; TCS Agentic Claims Processing Solution on AWS; TCS Manufacturing AI for Agentic Futures; TCS AI VX Studio on AWS; TCS Automotive Cockpit Solution Suite. 

Expanded 20-year partnership with SAP, with a five-year deal to support the **“SAP runs SAP”** philosophy and this collaboration shall leverage transformative potential of AI and cloud to unlock new revenue streams, accelerate business growth, and deliver differentiated experiences for enterprise and their customers globally. The engagement will be anchored by four strategic Centers of Excellence for Generative AI, Business Technology Platform, Business Data Cloud, and Customer Experience. 

TCS successfully conducted the first edition of the **tcs**<sup>**AI**</sup> **Hackathon 2025** , which became the world’s largest and most inclusive AI hackathon with participation from over 281,000 employees across 58 countries. The initiative reinforced TCS’ commitment to building an AI-first culture and enabling employees to develop innovative solutions through collaborative, hands-on experiences. 

**TCS CodeVita** set a new milestone with 146,922 participants, surpassing its 2021 **Guinness World Record’s** title to remain the world’s largest coding championship, promoting coding as a competitive discipline and nurturing problem-solving talent. 

Expanded partnership with **Google Cloud** by adopting its next generation agentic AI platform, Gemini Enterprise. This partnership will empower TCS’ workforce to build agentic AI solutions to redefine Human + AI workflows. 

Announced the launch of an **AI Experience Zone and Design Studio in London** , reaffirming its continued strategic investment across the UK. Further, with its continued investment in creating employment and supporting talent development, TCS will create **5,000 new jobs** across the UK over the next three years. 

AI is fundamentally redefining the value that professional services firms offer, and Gemini Enterprise provides TCS with a single, integrated platform that unifies enterprise knowledge and empowers every employee to become a force multiplier for the business. Together, we are helping our joints global customers integrate this best-in-class technology to accelerate their ability to build differentiated industry solutions and realize business value faster.

Quarter 2 

Quarter 1 

Signed a strategic partnership with **Tryg** , a leading Scandinavian non-life insurance company that operates in Denmark, Sweden, and Norway, for a seven-year €550M agreement. TCS will propel Tryg’s **‘United Towards 27’** vision of simplifying its IT operations and building the capacity to develop new digital solutions. The expanded partnership will create an opportunity for TCS to streamline a historically complex IT landscape, simplify operating models across geographies, and embed automation and AI to enhance efficiency in every element of the IT operations value chain. 

###### Collaborated with **MIT Sloan** 

###### **Management Review** to launch a 

research series on the next phase of human–AI collaboration in enterprises. As enterprises worldwide invest in AI-led transformation, this study examines emerging paradigms shaping AI adoption. It highlights the rise of **Intelligent Choice Architectures** - AI-driven systems that combine generative and predictive capabilities to create and refine decision options, enabling enterprises to deliver better, more informed outcomes through redesigned decision environments. 

Launched **Chiplet-based System Engineering Services** , to help semiconductor companies push the boundaries of traditional chip design. By using chiplets, TCS is enabling chipmakers to deliver faster, efficient, and more powerful processors amid growing global demand for advanced semiconductors. 

Partnered with **Qualcomm** to set up the **‘TCS Innovation Lab’** , a space for co-innovation in Bengaluru. TCS and Qualcomm will co-create smart, scalable, and sustainable Edge AI capabilities. Leveraging Qualcomm® platforms, the collaboration will help industries transition towards software-driven systems that are more efficient and resilient. 

Opened flagship **TCS Interactive Design Studio** in New York City. Located in midtown Manhattan, the multidisciplinary center has been established to help clients create iconic products, services, and experiences that are seamless and unified across digital ecosystems. 

**Milind Lakkad** concluded an exceptional 38-year journey with TCS on 30 September. Having joined the Company as a trainee in 1987, he went on to lead the manufacturing business group before serving as CHRO, leaving behind a distinguished legacy of leadership, talent development and organisational transformation. 

**TCS and IBM collaborated** with the **Government of Andhra Pradesh** to install India’s largest quantum computer and further develop India’s quantum industry as part of the new, first of a kind, **Quantum Valley Tech Park** , currently being built in the capital city of Amaravati. 

At a special ceremony in New Delhi during the **TCS Accelerating India event** , the Company launched three Indiafocused offerings that are sovereign by design, secure by default, and sustainable for the future. Designed to accelerate India’s AI-led transformation, TCS has launched **TCS** 

Expanded SDV capabilities with **two new Automotive Delivery Centers in Germany – located in Munich and VillingenSchwenningen as well as an engineering center in Romania.** These new hubs mark a strategic move to help TCS’ global automotive clients accelerate their transition to next-gen mobility solutions. 

Announced a strategic partnership with **Vianai Systems,** enabling TCS customers to access Vianai’s **hila Platform** , a next-gen tool for real-time data insights. TCS will customise the platform for financial institutions and other industries, leveraging its conversational capabilities to enhance core business functions such as CRM, sales and supply chain management across sectors. 

**SovereignSecure**<sup>**TM**</sup> **Cloud, TCS DigiBOLT**<sup>**TM**</sup> **,** and **TCS Cyber Defense Suite.**

# **Financial Capital**<sup>**1**</sup> 

Capital 1 

Financial capital remains a key enabler of TCS’ strategy, providing resilience and flexibility to fund innovation, invest ahead of demand, and support stakeholders through cycles. Strong profitability, disciplined allocation, and prudent cash management underpin the Company’s ability to scale capabilities and create long-term value. 

###### **Key outcomes** 

Operational Strong 

**Operational** excellence resulting in industry leading margins and return ratios 

**Prudent** working capital management leading to consistently high cash conversion 

###### **Sustained** investments in AI-led 

engineering, a highly skilled and scalable talent base, differentiated solutions, and a strong partner ecosystem 

**Broad** based client additions across revenue bands 

**Strong** TCV and continued momentum in large deals, with clients increasingly committing to long term, multi-year, multi-million dollar partnerships that reflect the trust they place in TCS to deliver certainty and value at scale over extended transformation journeys. 

1 GRI 201-1 

###### **Revenue Trend** ( H crore) 

267,021 240,893 255,324 225,458 191,754 10.2% CAGR FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

In FY 2026, TCS achieved a year-over year revenue growth of 4.6%, led by focused and effective execution. 

On a constant currency basis, revenue declined by 2.4%. The decline was largely a result of one of the Company’s large transformation programmes in India coming to an end this year. 

Over the past five financial years, the company recorded a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 10.2%, highlighting the resilience and scalability of its business model. 

###### **Earnings per share** (Amount in H ) 

 145.99 134.19 127.74 115.19 103.62 10.3% CAGR FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

Over the past five fiscal years, TCS has steadily increased its Earnings Per Share (EPS), recording a compound annual growth rate of 10.3%. This consistent upward trend demonstrates not only the company’s strengthening profits but also its dedication to providing sustained value for shareholders. 

###### **Shareholder Payouts** ( H crore) 

103.3% 99.2% 99.8% 93.9% 47,445 80.9% Shareholder Payout 45,588 (including Dividend, 42,079 Special Dividend, Buyback and Taxes) 39,799 38,010 Shareholder Payout ratio (Including special dividend) FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

TCS remains firmly committed to shareholders through a consistent and shareholder friendly dividend policy, while prudently expanding investments under the ‘Build-Partner-Acquire’ framework to strengthen long-term growth. Based on the company’s performance, the Board of Directors have declared three interim dividends of ₹11 each, a special dividend of ₹46, and recommended a final dividend of ₹31 (pending shareholders’ approval at this AGM), for a total of ₹110 per share for FY 2026. TCS has consistently declared dividend every quarter since its listing, complemented by three bonus issues and five buyback offers. 

###### **Operating Profit Trend** ( H crore) 

25.3% 25.0% 24.1% 24.6% 24.3% 66,838 59,311 62,165 54,237 48,453 Operating Profit Operating Margin FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

In FY 2026, TCS reported an operating margin of 25.0%, an improvement of 70 basis points compared to FY 2025, and was at a 4-year high. 

Improvement of business mix, productivity and realisation, rebalancing of pyramid along with favourable currency movements helped the expansion of margins. 

These were offset by investments in talent and capability building, and other investments for strengthening ecosystem partnerships and increasing Go-to-Market pipeline. 

###### **Operating Cash Flow and Cash Conversion** ( H crore) 

105.9% 104.2% 100.7% 99.6% 96.6% 48,908 52,094 41,965 44,338 39,949 Operating Cash Flow Cash Conversion FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

TCS has demonstrated a strong cash conversion ratio, which reflects its solid financial position and effective generation of operational cash. 

A robust cash balance allows TCS to invest and broaden TCS play across building capabilities, partnering with ecosystem players and acquiring capabilities; at speed and scale. 

TCS will continue its investments to maximise growth. 

**Return on Equity** (%) 

51.3% 52.2% 51.4% 46.9% 43.4% Best in class RoE FY 2022 FY 2023 FY 2024 FY 2025 FY 2026 

TCS’s consistent, industry-leading Return on Equity demonstrates its strong profitability and efficient resource management. This performance underscores the company’s financial discipline, operational excellence, and prudent allocation of shareholder capital. Moving forward, TCS remains committed to making strategic investments while sustaining its leading return ratios.

# **Social Capital** 

###### **Building Trust across Stakeholders** 

###### **Relationship spanning decades** 

**TCS being the top large IT service provider in Europe for customer satisfaction in 2025/2026 is outstanding. The company has built on twelve prior years of ranking first for customer satisfaction in Europe and clearly showcases a clientcentric approach and commitment to excellence. Our survey highlights their consistent delivery of value across vital domains for companies navigating complex digital transformations. It also demonstrates TCS’ ability to be a trusted partner, helping European enterprises navigate the changing digital environment with confidence and achieve tangible business outcomes.** 

TCS aims to build enduring trust across its stakeholder ecosystem through long-term value creation and sustained collaboration with customers, suppliers, investors, analysts, ecosystem partners, and communities.

###### **Customers** 

#### 250+ 

Customer centricity is foundational to TCS’ business strategy and underpins the Company’s approach to creating long term value. TCS continues to be regarded as a trusted partner, combining deep contextual understanding, domain expertise, and advanced technology capabilities to help customers navigate complex challenges. This customer focused approach is reflected in consistently high levels of customer satisfaction, with TCS recording a 94.4% customer satisfaction score in FY 2026, along with strong deal momentum supported by US$ 40.7 billion of TCV in FY 2026, including five mega deal wins.

TCS’ Pace Ports and Studios enable experience-led engagements by embedding Rapid AI Builds into customer interactions, helping identify high impact challenges and contextual AI solutions. 

###### **Suppliers** 

TCS emphasises responsible sourcing through transparency, sustainability, and ethical business practices. All suppliers must adhere to TCS’ Supplier Code of Conduct (SCoC), Health, Safety and Environment (HSE) requirements, and relevant policies, which guide the Company’s procurement practices. The responsible sourcing programme continues to cover 100% of TCS’ suppliers. 

###### **Ecosystem Partners and Alliances** 

TCS strengthened its partner ecosystem across hyperscalers, enterprise platforms, deep tech companies, and industry collaborators to support customer transformation and long-term value creation. In FY 2026, the Company advanced collaborations with OpenAI to support enterprise AI adoption, co-develop industry specific AI solutions, and enable AI infrastructure development in India, alongside partnerships with semiconductor players such as AMD. 

During the same period, TCS deepened strategic collaborations with Microsoft, Google Cloud, NVIDIA, and ServiceNow, while expanding partnerships with Salesforce through targeted acquisitions to enhance advisory, implementation, and managed services capabilities. In addition, long standing industry partnerships with ABB and Jaguar Land Rover continued to strengthen capabilities across digital engineering, industrial AI, and infrastructure modernisation.

###### **Analysts** 

TCS’ Analyst Relations programme focuses on building mature and productive relationships with the analyst community, strengthening brand reputation, market influence, and strategic insight. 

> As a testament to its market leadership, **TCS was rated a Leader in 165 competitive positioning assessments** conducted by leading analyst firms, evaluating strategy, vision, and delivery capabilities. These independent recognitions reflect TCS’ strong leadership across key services, industries, and products and platforms, with analyst commentary highlighting its AI-led transformation, contextual depth, and strengths in advisory and innovation. 

###### **Investors** 

TCS continues to excel in investor outreach by fostering transparent engagement and providing comprehensive disclosures, effectively communicating strategy, risks, and opportunities to investors, ensuring fair stock valuation. 

During the year, TCS hosted an **‘Analyst Day’** to engage with equity analysts, outlining its AI-led aspiration supported by five pillar transformation to lead in the AI era. The event featured leadership presentations, AI rapid build immersion sessions and showcased customer case studies across the Infrastructure to Intelligence stack, reinforcing TCS as a full stack AI services partner for enterprises.

###### Education 

**TCS go Innovate Together (goIT)** is a no-cost digital innovation and career readiness programme designed to equip students from diverse backgrounds with future-ready skills. It encourages them to become innovators and address real-world societal challenges. 

CT is not just a subject, it is a way of thinking, a way of contributing. Our students didn’t just learn how to solve problems; they learned what it means to be responsible Equipping students with the skills, citizens. Supported by the TCS IMF confidence and mindset necessary to team, this programme showed pursue careers of the future with TCS go how education rooted in real-world Innovate Together (goIT)relevance can empower students to become innovative problem solvers in their communities. 

Since its launch in 2009, goIT has positively impacted **396,342** students in its growing presence across **67** countries.

TCS Youth Employment Programme (YEP) addresses youth unemployment by upskilling students from marginalised communities, equipping them with industry relevant skills, and facilitating placement opportunities. ###### Entrepreneurship 

###### **TCS Women Empowerment** 

**Programme (WEP)** focuses on providing access to essential services in the hardest-to-reach rural locations in India by creating digital entrepreneurs within marginalised communities. 

Since its inception in 2014, WEP has created **1,866** entrepreneurs of which **1,533** are active, comprising **87%** women and **52%** from marginalised communities, serving **8,371 villages** across **42 districts** by offering essential services like document generation, government scheme enrolment, online form filling, and banking transactions, among others. 

Since its inception in 2010, the programme has enhanced the socio-economic status of **186,241** youth and families, provided youth access to opportunities in **36** states and union territories, empowered **108,249** young women and **50,885** people from underserved communities, and placed **44,002** students. 

> In FY 2026, YEP trained **1,098** youths and facilitated gainful employment of **164** students in information technology and other domains. 

This journey has not only helped me gain some exposure to a professional work environment, it has also allowed me to build skills that are really important for my career growth. 

The best thing about this programme is that it covers all the aspects that a fresher needs to crack an interview or to succeed in the corporate world. From business communication to digital skills, aptitude to insightful master sessions, they have covered everything in it well. 

This programme has really prepared me for the real world. Additionally, the guidance that has been provided throughout this journey has boosted my confidence and given clarity to my career, thank you!

###### Literacy 

**TCS Literacy Programme** also consists of the **‘Each One Empowers One’** initiative that gives TCS and Tata group employees the opportunity to volunteer and empower nonliterates in their community. In FY 2026, this digital platform has over **3,725** volunteers with **12,415** beneficiaries. 

Since its inception in 2000, TCS Literacy Programme has empowered over **5.1 million** beneficiaries and **493,928** facilitators. In FY 2026, TCS Literacy Programme empowered **2.7 million** learners. 

As a Mission for Elimination of Poverty in Municipal Areas (MEPMA) Resource Person, I saw that of the 350 Women SHG members that participated actively, many didn’t fully understand group processes. Wanting to do more, I volunteered with Akshara Andhra and began literacy sessions for 10 women using the ULLAS primer and TCS videos. Over time, they started checking records themselves, speaking up, and making confident decisions—strengthening trust, transparency, and ownership within their SHG.

###### Hours of Purpose by Employees (HOPE) 

Building capacity and capability for non-profits to create greater social impact with TCS **Tech4HOPE.** 

TCS Tech4HOPE is a skill-based employee volunteering initiative that delivers pro bono technology and advisory support to nonprofits, enabling sustainable community impact. 

Since inception, Tech4HOPE has created **US $21.1 million** in ‘social value.’ 

In FY 2026, Tech4HOPE delivered pro bono digital services to **81** nonprofits, completing **51** projects and advancing 30 projects in progress. Through **24,912** hours of skillbased volunteering, the initiative generated an estimated social value of US **$5.5** million.

###### Conservation and restoration of traditional water systems across India 

Rajon ki Baoli, built in 1506 during the Lodi era, is a four-level stepwell situated within the Mehrauli Archaeological Park. Once a vital water source, its revival called for more than conservation - it demanded systemic restoration. 

**A three-pronged approach was adopted to improve water quality:** 

###### **Biological** 

Reintroduction of aquatic life 

To prevent groundwater contamination, TCS Foundation collaborated for restoration and rejuvenation of Rajon ki Baoli. Overflow from the local Sewage Treatment Plant (STP) was redirected, stormwater networks were repaired, and nearby settlements were connected to the sewerage grid. 

###### **Chemical** 

Use of alums for water clarification 

###### **Electromechanical** 

Aerator jets and submersible pumps to oxygenate and maintain water levels 

The front plaza was regraded and paved with grass-jointed stone over a sand bed; a simple, sustainable intervention to reduce sedimentation into the baoli.

Every year, millions of Indians suffer burn injuries, most of them preventable. Behind these numbers are families who lose not only health but also livelihoods, education, and identity. 

surgeries were assisted in FY 2026 and

The TCS Foundation supported an organisation, founded by a burn survivor. This organisation offers holistic rehabilitation, which includes reconstructive surgeries, awareness and counselling to help survivors regain confidence and reintegrate into society. TCS Foundation provided financial assistance for free surgeries and postsurgery rehabilitation of burn survivors. 

According to the World Health Organisation, India records nearly 70 lakh burn injuries annually, with over 1.4 lakh deaths each year ¹. That is one life lost every four minutes. What makes it worse is that four out of every five burn victims are women or children², and most cases occur in low-income or rural households.

###### Establishing support for skilling and innovation needs 

TCS Foundation supported a project to launch a Centre of Excellence (CoE) in Gorakhpur, Uttar Pradesh. The CoE aims to nurture and empower talent from Eastern Uttar Pradesh (Purvanchal) in emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence (AI), drone technology, 3D printing (additive manufacturing), space 

###### Creating a network of schools and after-school centres 

The aim is to create a network of schools, teachers, and after-school centres by creating a learning infrastructure and build a network of teacher training university and online certification programmes. The programme is a blended teacher training course that prepares educators to equip students for the 21<sup>st</sup> century by using incubated schools as practical training environments. The programme was built to supply the system with a teaching and coaching workforce that is better prepared to reinvent education. The programme runs two tracks - one for teachers and one for coaches - tailored to strengthen instructional practice and coaching leadership. 

system across 120 acres. The project includes a new sewage treatment plant to address chronic overflow and irregular connections, ensuring hygienic, futureready infrastructure that can adapt to evolving needs while preserving the sanctity and sustainability of this centuries-old place. 

The CoE has been set up to support the skilling and innovation needs of Eastern UP, where access to advanced technological infrastructure and sustained industry-academia collaboration is still emerging. By creating a centralised platform focused on selected high-impact technology areas, the CoE aims to strengthen regional research and innovation efforts, support entrepreneurship, and enhance employability, while contributing to broader regional priorities and national development objectives. 

technology, and cybersecurity, and medical technology (MedTech). 

Established with the support of the TCS Foundation and operationally managed by the institute, the Centre of Excellence is envisioned as a future-ready hub for advanced learning, applied research, and innovation. Bringing together emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence, drone technology, and more, the Centre focuses on hands-on learning through modular laboratories, real-world projects, and applied problem-solving. The CoE aims to incubate 100 startups. 

**~~Story 1~~** A first-year teacher, part of the teacher track in this programme who through the programmes’ Virtual Elective session, her first experience with online learning, participated in electives on Whole Child Development and Building Vocabulary in the foundational years. She found the format flexible and practical, enabling immediate classroom application, and gained a holistic approach to lesson planning by integrating cognitive, language, physical, and socialemotional development, an experience she described as rich and impactful. 

**~~Story 2~~** It takes a village to raise a child, a phrase that is often heard. The founders of a community-based school translated this idea into everyday practice. Their community board is one of the most deeply involved - not just in decision-making, but in the lives of each child. They actively discuss every student’s circumstances, often visiting homes and stepping in with support wherever it is needed. 

With just four local teachers, a single school campus in the entire village, and a committed community, this school is redefining what community-based learning can look like - especially in one of the most conflict-affected regions of the country.

# **Natural Capital** 

TCS recognises environmental protection as essential to long-term value creation, reflecting the Tata Group’s commitment to environmental stewardship, embodied in Project Aalingana. TCS focuses on minimising environmental impacts by embracing Aalingana’s interlinked pillars: driving the decarbonisation of its businesses and value chain; applying a systemic, circular economy approach to reduce resource use and waste; and preserving and restoring the natural environment. TCS’ Natural Capital approach emphasises efficient use of resources, protection of ecosystems, and integration of environmental considerations into operational decision-making.

**26%** reduction in Scope 3 compared to base year 

TCS also commits to reduce absolute scope 3 emissions **35%** by FY 2034 from a FY 2020 base year.

###### **Water Management** 

###### **Waste Management** 

TCS focuses on reducing freshwater withdrawal through recycle and reuse initiatives, sewage treatment, enhancing water-use efficiency, and rainwater harvesting. Special emphasis is placed on managing water risks in water-stressed locations, supported by governance mechanisms, monitoring and employee engagement. Green building design promotes sustainable water practices including water recharge across few of the Company campuses. All new campuses have been designed for higher water efficiency, rainwater harvesting and treatment and recycling of sewage. 

TCS focuses on waste reduction at source, segregation, recycling, and safe disposal across its operations. TCS is committed to the circular economy principles, including minimisation of waste disposed to landfill, responsible management of e-waste, battery waste and hazardous waste by engagement with authorised recyclers and resellers for appropriate recovery/reuse. TCS is also committed to advancing zero waste to landfill practices across its operations. **9** TCS locations are already certified for ‘ **Zero Waste to Landfill** ’ with 99*% of waste diverted away from landfill and more locations are planned. 

###### **Initiatives** 

###### **Initiatives** 

- Maintenance of rainwater harvesting structures, groundwater recharging structures and recharge pits 

 - Installation of recycled plastic benches in recreational areas 

 - Installation of Organic Waste Converters 

- Installation of water efficient fixtures (water efficient faucets, sensor based taps etc.) 

 - Disposal of non-recyclable/ unsegregated waste through waste to energy plant at few campuses. 

- Maintenance of sewage treatment plant and water recycling infrastructure 

 - % of waste generated that is % compliance to management recovered through recycling practices for regulated wastes like and reuse hazardous waste, e-waste etc. 76.4% 100% 

- Installation of digital water metres and its calibration 

- Water quality monitoring 

- Water treatment plants

###### **Biodiversity** 

Tata Group’s Project Aalingana emphasises the need to manage company’s impacts and dependencies on biodiversity and enhance their performance on biodiversity conservation. TCS Nature and Biodiversity policy outlines TCS’ commitment and plan of action to conserve, preserve and enhance nature and biodiversity. 

During the year, the Company has conducted biodiversity assessment of campuses and raised employee awareness through sessions, webinars and nature walks/ trails. TCS’ flora and fauna conservation initiatives at campus level include:

### **Shareholder Connect** 

###### **Key Actions for Shareholders** 

The Company remains committed to delivering shareholder-centric solutions that address specific requirements through the adoption of technology-driven initiatives, strengthening shareholder engagement and enhancing awareness. A dedicated investor services team promptly addresses shareholder queries and facilitates constructive engagement, promoting transparency, trust, and long-term relationships. These efforts reinforce the Company’s commitment to ensuring that shareholders remain well-informed, valued, and integral to its sustained growth and success. Key initiatives and other relevant information are outlined below: 

- **1 Mandatory KYC Updation** 

Shareholders are requested to update KYC details in order to receive timely payment of dividend(s) and avoid any withholding of dividend(s). For detailed procedure refer note no. 11 of the AGM Notice. 

- **2 Dematerialisation of shares** 

###### **Initiatives** 

###### **Technology-driven** 

- The Company has developed eTDS Portal for shareholders, providing a secure self-service view of their consolidated dividend and tax information. The Portal offers financial year wise and quarter-wise details of dividend income, including particulars of Tax Deducted at Source (TDS), shareholding in TCS across all demat accounts and/or physical folios and facility for downloading TDS certificates. Link to access the portal: <u>https://on.tcs.com/eTDS-portal.</u> 

- Web-based query resolution systems: 

- Swayam is an application to view records, download documents, raise queries etc. Link to access the application: <u>https://swayam.in.mpms.mufg.com.</u> 

- iDIA is a chatbot which provides quick guidance on queries raised or directs shareholders to appropriate channel to resolve their queries. 

 - Link to the chatbot: <u>https://in.mpms.mufg.com.</u> 

are available at 

###### **Unclaimed Dividends** 

- Voluntary reminder letters and SMS alerts are sent to claim unclaimed dividends before its statutory transfer to the Investor Education and Protection Fund (IEPF) Authority. Details of unclaimed dividends are available at <u>https://on.tcs.com/TCS-IEPF.</u> 

- A special cycle of remittance was conducted twice during the year to remit unclaimed dividends directly into the updated bank account of shareholders before transfer of such dividends to IEPF. 

- KYC letters are sent to encourage shareholders to update their KYC details and dematerialize their shares held in physical form. 

###### **Creating Awareness and Enhancing Shareholder Engagement** 

- Personalised TDS calculations on dividend are provided in the e-credit intimations sent to shareholders. 

- Quarterly financial results are sent to shareholders at their registered email address. 

- Periodic updates through earnings calls, press conferences, press releases and other investor communications are provided under the ‘Investors’ section of the Company’s website. 

- Investor FAQs covering aspects such as unclaimed dividend, IEPF, KYC updation, taxation of dividend distribution, opening of a special window for lodgement of share transfer requests and DigiLocker facility etc., are made available at <u>https://on.tcs.com/Investor-FAQs.</u> 

As per SEBI mandate, requests for transfer, transmission and transposition of securities, are processed only in dematerialised form. Accordingly, Shareholders are encouraged to dematerialize their shares held in physical form to ensure faster, safer and more efficient services. Shareholders may contact the Company/RTA for dematerialisation of such shares.
